channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US-Russia talks to end war in Ukraine begin Tuesday in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Top officials from the United States and Russian will hold talks in Saudi Arabia toward ending the war in Ukraine on Tuesday, as the Trump administration seeks to negotiate an end to the nearly three-year-old conflict.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: European leaders regroup in Paris for strategy huddle after Trump diplomatic blitz on Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron called leaders from key European Union nations and the United Kingdom to his ornate Elysee Palace on Monday for an emergency meeting on how to react to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here are some takeaways from the first month of Trump's Mideast diplomacy
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — When Hamas threatened to call off the planned release of three Israeli hostages last week, U.S. President Donald Trump stepped into the picture with an unexpected ultimatum.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Russian and American officials will hold talks on ending the Ukraine war without Kyiv
item.description: The Kremlin says that senior American and Russian officials will hold talks on improving their ties and negotiating an end to the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia plans to deport 3 violent criminals to Nauru
item.description: Australia will soon deport three violent criminals including a convicted murderer to Nauru even though they are not citizens of the tiny Pacific atoll, after an Australian court ruled that dangerous immigrants can no longer be imprisoned indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio spars with Brennan over Vance’s Munich speech
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio sparred with CBS host Margaret Brennan over Vice President Vance’s speech in Munich.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Senate Democrats split over how to combat Trump's USAID shutdown
item.description: Senate Democrats are split over how to push back against President Trump’s attempts to slash the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), an embattled front line in the larger fight against the administration’s effort to overhaul the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Shaheen says Trump administration gave 'mixed message' on Ukraine
item.description: Sen. Jeanne Shaheen (D-N.H.) said in an interview Sunday that the Trump administration has recently given a "mixed message" on Ukraine. “Well, I think it was a mixed [message] coming from the administration,” Shaheen told CBS News’s Margaret Brennan on “Face the Nation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Suspect in fatal stabbing in Austria had an 'Islamic terror motive,' officials say
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian authorities said Sunday the stabbing of six people that left a 14-year-old boy dead was carried out by a man with possible connections to the Islamic State group who appeared to have acted alone.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Trump urged to confront Iranian regime over repression of Christians
item.description: Iran’s repression of Christians continues to grow, according to advocacy groups and analysts. Earlier this month the regime re-arrested two men who had previously been jailed due to their faith.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly Austria stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkish opposition party delegation meets with Kurdish leader in Iraq as part of PKK peace efforts
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — A Turkish opposition party delegation arrived in Iraq’s semiautonomous Kurdish region Sunday against the backdrop of peace efforts between Ankara and a banned Kurdish separatist movement in Turkey.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA and ÇINAR KIPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fans in Montreal loudly boo US anthem prior to Americans' 4 Nations Face-Off game vs. Canada
item.description: Fans in Montreal loudly booed the U.S. national anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off game between the Americans and Canada on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Whyno, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: PRETORIA, South Africa (AP) — Some white South Africans showed support for President Donald Trump on Saturday and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: ALFONSO NQUNJANA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: American accused of smuggling drugs taken into Russian custody
item.description: A court in Russia ordered the 30-day detention of a U.S. citizen suspected of drug smuggling, Moscow courts press service reported Saturday, per the Associated Press. Kalob Wayne Byers, 28, was apprehended by customs officials at Vnukovo airport in Moscow during a baggage inspection.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Students in Iran continue protests over 19-year-old’s murder on campus for second day
item.description: Student protesters in Iran called out officials at the University of Tehran for a second day on Saturday following the murder of a business student on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marco Rubio arrives in Israel on first trip to Middle East as U.S. secretary of state
item.description: Marco Rubio has arrived in Israel on his first trip to the Middle East as U.S. secretary of state; he will reportedly discuss Gaza and the aftermath of the Hamas attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas complete their latest exchange as ceasefire's first phase has just 2 weeks left
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel and Hamas completed the sixth exchange of hostages and Palestinian prisoners on Saturday with just over two weeks remaining in their fragile Gaza ceasefire's initial phase, and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio landed in Israel to begin a Mideast tour.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, MELANIE LIDMAN and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases over 300 Palestinian prisoners after receiving 3 more hostages
item.description: Israel and Hamas completed their sixth exchange of hostages and prisoners on Saturday after their ceasefire survived its latest crisis. The Palestinian militant group was going to delay the exchange because Israel wasn’t letting the agreed-upon humanitarian aid into the devastated Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doctors order 'absolute rest' for Pope Francis while he's being treated for a respiratory infection
item.description: ROME (AP) — Doctors on Saturday prescribed “absolute rest” for Pope Francis and modified the treatment for his respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said, a day after the 88-year-old pope was admitted to a hospital following a weeklong bout of bronchitis.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A 2-year-old girl and her mother have died after the car-ramming attack in Munich
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A 2-year-old girl and her mother have died two days after they were injured in the car-ramming attack on a labor union demonstration in Munich, police said Saturday. A 24-year-old Afghan man who came to Germany as an asylum-seeker was arrested immediately after the attack on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu will reportedly hold a security meeting at Trump's deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu is expected to hold a security meeting at 12 p.m. eastern, President Trump's deadline for Hamas to release the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages in exchange for more than 300 prisoners as part of ceasefire deal with Israel
item.description: Hamas freed three more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian militants name 3 Israeli men to be freed in next hostage release
item.description: The families of Israeli hostages still held by the Hamas militant group on Friday welcomed the “joyous news” that three men, all taken from the same kibbutz on Oct. 7, 2023, are expected to be freed on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners held in Israeli jails.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine selling arms to cartels? Tucker Carlson claims rebutted
item.description: Carlson claims U.S. intelligence agencies know about the billions in transactions to arm “our actual enemies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's taunting of Canada will pay dividends: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: “Those of use who live in the real world — not the ideological, crazy world — we know it’s just hyperbole.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation
